project.name: agent-orchestrator
project.description: Lightweight two-part system for autonomous software development. An LLM planner organizes work from project management tools, and a deterministic state machine executes that work by dispatching headless coding agents through YAML-defined workflows.

conventions: Use Zod for all schema validation and type inference (z.infer<typeof Schema>)
Use named exports, not default exports
Use path.resolve and path.join for all file paths
Use fs/promises (not sync fs) for all file I/O
Error handling: throw typed errors, let callers decide recovery
Use console-level logging via the logger utility, not raw console.log
All config paths are relative to the orchestrator repo root and resolved at load time
State files are the contract between planner and runner — planner writes, runner reads/updates

coreTypes.note: These are the canonical type shapes all modules must conform to. Task 02 (core-types) produces the Zod schemas. Other tasks should code against these shapes.

PlanFile.name: string
PlanFile.tickets: Array<{ ticketId: string, order: number, blockedBy: string[] }>

TicketState.title: string
TicketState.phaseHistory: Array<{ phase: string, status: string, startedAt: string, completedAt: string | null, output?: string }>

PhaseDefinition.promptTemplate?: string (for agent phases, filename in prompts/)

tasks.name: Project Scaffolding
tasks.description: Set up the TypeScript project foundation: package.json, tsconfig.json, .gitignore, and create all directories. This task creates the skeleton that all other tasks build on.
tasks.prompt:
Create the project scaffolding for a TypeScript CLI called 'agent-orchestrator'. Create the following files:

1. **package.json**:
   - name: agent-orchestrator
   - type: module
   - bin: { "orchestrator": "./dist/cli.js" }
   - scripts: { "build": "tsc", "dev": "tsx src/cli.ts", "start": "node dist/cli.js" }
   - dependencies: yaml, nunjucks, commander, chalk, zod, chokidar
   - devDependencies: typescript, tsx, @types/node, @types/nunjucks

2. **tsconfig.json**:
   - target: ES2022, module: Node16, moduleResolution: Node16
   - outDir: ./dist, rootDir: ./src
   - strict: true, esModuleInterop: true, skipLibCheck: true
   - declaration: true

3. **.gitignore**:
   - node_modules/, dist/, state/, logs/, *.log

4. **orchestrator.yaml** (default config):
   ```yaml
   defaultAgent: claude
   agents:
     claude:
       command: claude
       defaultArgs:
         - "--dangerously-skip-permissions"
     codex:
       command: codex
       defaultArgs:
         - "--approval-mode"
         - "never"
   stateDir: ./state
   logDir: ./logs
   workflowDir: ./workflows
   promptDir: ./prompts
   scriptDir: ./scripts
   pollInterval: 10
   maxConcurrency: 3
   ghCommand: gh
   ```

5. Create empty directories (with .gitkeep where appropriate):
   - src/core/
   - src/agents/
   - src/phases/
   - src/utils/
   - workflows/
   - prompts/
   - scripts/
   - skills/planner/
   - skills/workflows/
   - skills/providers/linear/
   - skills/providers/github-issues/
   - schemas/

Run `npm install` after creating package.json.

tasks.name: Zod Schemas & Types
tasks.description: Define all Zod schemas and TypeScript types that form the contract between all modules. This is the single source of truth for data shapes.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/core/types.ts` with all Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types for the agent orchestrator. Use `z.infer<typeof Schema>` for all type exports. Use named exports.

Define the following schemas:

1. **AgentConfigSchema**: { command: string, defaultArgs: string[] }

2. **OrchestratorConfigSchema**: {
   defaultAgent: string,
   agents: z.record(string, AgentConfigSchema),
   stateDir: string,
   logDir: string,
   workflowDir: string,
   promptDir: string,
   scriptDir: string,
   pollInterval: number (default 10),
   maxConcurrency: number (default 3),
   ghCommand: string (default 'gh')
   }

3. **PlanTicketEntrySchema**: { ticketId: string, order: number, blockedBy: string[] (default []) }

4. **PlanStatusSchema**: z.enum(['active', 'paused', 'complete'])

5. **PlanFileSchema**: {
   id: string,
   name: string,
   createdAt: string,
   createdBy: string,
   repo: string,
   workflow: string,
   agent: string | null (default null),
   worktreeRoot: string,
   status: PlanStatusSchema,
   tickets: PlanTicketEntrySchema[]
   }

6. **TicketStatusSchema**: z.enum(['queued', 'ready', 'running', 'paused', 'complete', 'failed', 'needs_attention'])

7. **PhaseHistoryEntrySchema**: { phase: string, status: 'success' | 'failure' | 'skipped', startedAt: string, completedAt: string | null, output: string (optional) }

8. **TicketStateSchema**: {
   planId: string,
   ticketId: string,
   title: string,
   description: string,
   acceptanceCriteria: string[] (default []),
   linearUrl: string | null (default null),
   repo: string,
   workflow: string,
   branch: string,
   worktree: string,
   agent: string | null (default null),
   status: TicketStatusSchema,
   currentPhase: string,
   phaseHistory: PhaseHistoryEntrySchema[] (default []),
   context: z.record(string, string) (default {}),
   retries: z.record(string, number) (default {}),
   error: string | null (default null)
   }

9. **PhaseTypeSchema**: z.enum(['script', 'agent', 'poll', 'terminal'])

10. **PhaseDefinitionSchema**: {
    id: string,
    type: PhaseTypeSchema,
    command: string (optional),
    args: string[] (optional, default []),
    promptTemplate: string (optional),
    allowedTools: string[] (optional),
    maxTurns: number (optional),
    maxRetries: number (optional, default 0),
    agent: string | null (optional),
    intervalSeconds: number (optional),
    timeoutSeconds: number (optional),
    capture: z.record(string, string) (optional),
    notify: boolean (optional, default false),
    onSuccess: string (optional),
    onFailure: string (optional)
    }

11. **WorkflowDefinitionSchema**: { name: string, description: string (optional, default ''), phases: PhaseDefinitionSchema[] }

12. **WorkflowRegistryEntrySchema**: { name: string, file: string, description: string, tags: string[] (default []) }

Export both the schemas (e.g., `PlanFileSchema`) and inferred types (e.g., `type PlanFile = z.infer<typeof PlanFileSchema>`). Export everything as named exports.

tasks.name: Shell Execution Utility
tasks.description: Subprocess execution wrapper for running bash scripts and CLI commands. Used by phase executor, capture logic, and agent invoker.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/utils/shell.ts` — a utility for executing shell commands as child processes.

Export the following:

1. **ShellResult** interface: { stdout: string, stderr: string, exitCode: number }

2. **execCommand(command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string, timeout?: number, env?: Record<string, string> }): Promise<ShellResult>**
   - Uses child_process.spawn (import from 'node:child_process')
   - Captures stdout and stderr as strings
   - Resolves with ShellResult (does NOT reject on non-zero exit — caller decides)
   - Supports optional timeout (kills process with SIGTERM after timeout ms)
   - Merges provided env with process.env
   - Streams output to buffers, trims final whitespace

3. **execCommandStreaming(command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string, timeout?: number, env?: Record<string, string>, onStdout?: (chunk: string) => void, onStderr?: (chunk: string) => void }): Promise<ShellResult>**
   - Same as execCommand but also calls onStdout/onStderr callbacks for real-time output
   - Still accumulates full stdout/stderr in the result

Use 'node:child_process' for spawn. Keep it simple — no retries, no parsing, just clean subprocess execution.

tasks.name: Logger Utility
tasks.description: Per-ticket file logging plus console output with color. Each ticket gets its own log file, and the console shows a summary with phase transitions.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/utils/logger.ts` — a logging utility that writes to both per-ticket log files and the console.

Export the following:

1. **createLogger(logDir: string)** — factory function that returns a Logger instance. Creates logDir if it doesn't exist.

2. **Logger** class/object with methods:
   - **info(message: string, ticketId?: string): void** — Logs to console (chalk.blue prefix) and to ticket log file if ticketId provided
   - **warn(message: string, ticketId?: string): void** — chalk.yellow prefix
   - **error(message: string, ticketId?: string): void** — chalk.red prefix
   - **success(message: string, ticketId?: string): void** — chalk.green prefix
   - **phaseStart(ticketId: string, phaseId: string): void** — Logs phase start with timestamp
   - **phaseEnd(ticketId: string, phaseId: string, status: 'success' | 'failure', durationMs: number): void** — Logs phase completion with duration
   - **agentOutput(ticketId: string, output: string): void** — Writes agent stdout to the ticket's log file (not console)

Log file format: `{logDir}/{ticketId}.log`
Each line: `[ISO timestamp] [LEVEL] message`

Use chalk for console colors. Use fs/promises appendFile for log writes. Create a simple formatter — no heavy logging frameworks.

Console format: `[HH:MM:SS] [LEVEL] [ticketId?] message` with colored level tags.

tasks.name: Config Loader
tasks.description: Loads orchestrator.yaml, validates with Zod, resolves relative paths to absolute. Single source of runtime configuration.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/core/config.ts` — loads and validates the orchestrator configuration.

Imports: OrchestratorConfigSchema and OrchestratorConfig from './types.ts'. Uses 'yaml' package to parse YAML.

Export the following:

1. **loadConfig(configPath?: string): Promise<OrchestratorConfig>**
   - Default configPath: 'orchestrator.yaml' in current working directory
   - Reads the YAML file, parses it, validates with OrchestratorConfigSchema
   - Resolves all relative directory paths (stateDir, logDir, workflowDir, promptDir, scriptDir) to absolute paths based on the directory containing the config file
   - Throws a clear error if file not found or validation fails
   - Returns the validated config

2. **resolveAgent(config: OrchestratorConfig, phaseAgent: string | null | undefined, ticketAgent: string | null | undefined, planAgent: string | null | undefined): { command: string, defaultArgs: string[] }**
   - Implements the four-level agent priority chain: phase → ticket → plan → global default
   - Takes the first non-null value in that chain, falls back to config.defaultAgent
   - Looks up the resolved agent name in config.agents
   - Throws if the resolved agent name is not found in config.agents
   - Returns the AgentConfig (command + defaultArgs)

Keep it simple. No caching, no file watching. Just load, validate, resolve.

tasks.name: Template Renderer
tasks.description: Nunjucks-based template engine that renders prompt templates with ticket state variables and accumulated context.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/core/template.ts` — renders Nunjucks templates with variables from ticket state and context.

Imports: TicketState from './types.ts'. Uses 'nunjucks' package.

Export the following:

1. **createTemplateRenderer(promptDir: string)** — factory that returns a TemplateRenderer. Configures a Nunjucks environment with:
   - FileSystemLoader pointing at promptDir
   - autoescape: false (these are markdown templates, not HTML)
   - throwOnUndefined: false (missing variables render as empty string)

2. **TemplateRenderer** object with methods:
   - **render(templateFile: string, ticket: TicketState): string** — Renders the named template file with variables populated from the ticket. The template variable namespace should be flat and include:
     - All ticket fields directly: ticket_id, title, description, acceptance_criteria (joined with newlines), repo, branch, worktree, linear_url, plan_id
     - All keys from ticket.context spread in (so {{pr_url}}, {{git_diff_stat}}, etc. work)
     - A special `acceptance_criteria_list` variable that formats criteria as a markdown bullet list
   - **renderString(template: string, ticket: TicketState): string** — Same but renders an inline template string instead of a file. Useful for interpolating {{var}} in script args.

Note: Template files use {{variable}} Nunjucks syntax. The templates are markdown files in the prompts/ directory.

Keep it simple — just variable substitution, no custom filters or tags needed.

tasks.name: State Manager
tasks.description: Reads and writes plan and ticket state files from the state/ directory. Handles multi-plan scanning, dependency resolution, and status transitions.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/core/state.ts` — manages plan and ticket state files on disk.

Imports: PlanFile, PlanFileSchema, TicketState, TicketStateSchema from './types.ts'. Uses fs/promises and path.

Export the following:

1. **createStateManager(stateDir: string)** — factory that returns a StateManager. Creates stateDir if it doesn't exist.

2. **StateManager** object with methods:

   **Plan operations:**
   - **listPlans(): Promise<PlanFile[]>** — Scans stateDir for subdirectories, reads _plan.json from each, validates with Zod, returns all valid plans. Skips directories without _plan.json.
   - **getPlan(planId: string): Promise<PlanFile>** — Reads and validates state/{planId}/_plan.json. Throws if not found.
   - **savePlan(plan: PlanFile): Promise<void>** — Writes _plan.json to state/{plan.id}/. Creates directory if needed.

   **Ticket operations:**
   - **listTickets(planId: string): Promise<TicketState[]>** — Reads all .json files in state/{planId}/ except _plan.json. Validates each with Zod.
   - **getTicket(planId: string, ticketId: string): Promise<TicketState>** — Reads state/{planId}/{ticketId}.json. Throws if not found.
   - **saveTicket(ticket: TicketState): Promise<void>** — Writes to state/{ticket.planId}/{ticket.ticketId}.json. Validates before writing.
   - **updateTicket(planId: string, ticketId: string, updates: Partial<TicketState>): Promise<TicketState>** — Reads, merges updates, validates, saves, returns updated ticket.

   **Scheduling:**
   - **getReadyTickets(): Promise<TicketState[]>** — Scans ALL plans with status 'active'. For each plan, checks tickets with status 'queued' whose blockedBy dependencies have ALL reached 'complete' status. Returns those tickets (they should be transitioned to 'ready'). Also returns tickets already in 'ready' status.
   - **getRunningCount(): Promise<number>** — Counts tickets with status 'running' across all active plans.

   **Dependency resolution:**
   - **resolveDependencies(planId: string): Promise<string[]>** — For a given plan, finds all 'queued' tickets whose blockedBy are all 'complete', transitions them to 'ready', returns the ticketIds that were unblocked.

All file reads should handle race conditions gracefully (file might be written by planner while runner reads). Use try/catch on individual file reads and skip corrupted files with a warning.

JSON files are pretty-printed with 2-space indent when written.

tasks.name: Workflow Loader
tasks.description: Parses YAML workflow definition files, validates them, and provides lookup by name via the workflow registry.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/core/workflow.ts` — loads and caches YAML workflow definitions.

Imports: WorkflowDefinition, WorkflowDefinitionSchema, WorkflowRegistryEntry, WorkflowRegistryEntrySchema from './types.ts'. Uses 'yaml' package.

Export the following:

1. **createWorkflowLoader(workflowDir: string)** — factory that returns a WorkflowLoader.

2. **WorkflowLoader** object with methods:

   - **loadRegistry(): Promise<WorkflowRegistryEntry[]>** — Reads workflows/registry.yaml, parses it as an array of WorkflowRegistryEntry, validates with Zod. Throws if registry not found.

   - **loadWorkflow(name: string): Promise<WorkflowDefinition>** — Looks up the workflow name in the registry to find its file, reads and parses the YAML file from workflowDir, validates with Zod. Caches loaded workflows in memory (Map<string, WorkflowDefinition>). Throws if workflow not found in registry or file doesn't exist.

   - **getPhase(workflowName: string, phaseId: string): Promise<PhaseDefinition>** — Loads the workflow (from cache if available) and returns the specific phase. Throws if phase not found.

   - **getNextPhase(workflowName: string, currentPhaseId: string, outcome: 'success' | 'failure'): Promise<string | null>** — Returns the next phase ID based on the current phase's onSuccess/onFailure. Returns null if no transition defined (terminal).

   - **clearCache(): void** — Clears the in-memory workflow cache (useful if files change).

The workflow YAML structure:
```yaml
name: standard
description: Full implementation workflow
phases:
  - id: setup
    type: script
    command: setup-worktree.sh
    ...
```

Keep it simple — just load, validate, cache, lookup.

tasks.name: Agent Invoker
tasks.description: Abstraction layer for invoking headless coding agents (Claude Code, Codex). Translates a common interface into provider-specific CLI calls.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/agents/invoke.ts` — invokes headless coding agents as subprocesses.

Imports: ShellResult, execCommandStreaming from '../utils/shell.ts'. Import types as needed.

Export the following:

1. **AgentInvocation** interface:
   ```typescript
   {
     prompt: string;           // The rendered prompt to send to the agent
     cwd: string;              // Working directory (ticket worktree)
     allowedTools?: string[];  // Tool restrictions for the agent
     maxTurns?: number;        // Max agent turns
   }
   ```

2. **AgentResult** interface:
   ```typescript
   {
     stdout: string;
     stderr: string;
     exitCode: number;
     success: boolean;  // exitCode === 0
   }
   ```

3. **invokeAgent(agentConfig: { command: string, defaultArgs: string[] }, invocation: AgentInvocation, callbacks?: { onOutput?: (chunk: string) => void }): Promise<AgentResult>**

   This function builds CLI args based on the agent command:

   **For 'claude' command:**
   - Base: `claude -p "<prompt>" --output-format text`
   - Add defaultArgs from config
   - Add `--allowedTools` with comma-separated tool list if provided
   - Add `--max-turns <n>` if maxTurns provided
   - cwd is set via the spawn options

   **For 'codex' command:**
   - Base: `codex exec "<prompt>"`
   - Add defaultArgs from config
   - cwd is set via the spawn options

   **For any other command** (future agents):
   - Base: `<command> "<prompt>"`
   - Add defaultArgs from config
   - cwd via spawn options

   Uses execCommandStreaming to run the subprocess. Passes onOutput callback for real-time logging. Returns AgentResult with success derived from exitCode.

   Important: The prompt should be passed as a single argument (the full rendered prompt string). For Claude, use the -p flag. Handle shell escaping properly — pass args as an array to spawn, NOT as a shell string.

   Set a generous timeout (30 minutes default) since agent phases can take a while.

tasks.name: Phase Executor
tasks.description: Executes individual workflow phases by type (script, agent, poll, terminal). Handles capture logic, retries, and result reporting.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/phases/executor.ts` — executes individual workflow phases based on their type.

Imports from other modules:
- Types: PhaseDefinition, TicketState, OrchestratorConfig from '../core/types.ts'
- Shell: execCommand from '../utils/shell.ts'
- Config: resolveAgent from '../core/config.ts'
- Template: TemplateRenderer (the return type of createTemplateRenderer) from '../core/template.ts'
- Agent: invokeAgent, AgentResult from '../agents/invoke.ts'
- Logger: Logger type from '../utils/logger.ts'

Export the following:

1. **PhaseResult** interface:
   ```typescript
   {
     success: boolean;
     output: string;           // stdout or summary
     captured: Record<string, string>;  // Values captured by capture rules
     nextPhase: string | null; // Suggested next phase (from onSuccess/onFailure)
   }
   ```

2. **createPhaseExecutor(config: OrchestratorConfig, templateRenderer: TemplateRenderer, logger: Logger)** — factory returning a PhaseExecutor.

3. **PhaseExecutor** with method:
   - **execute(phase: PhaseDefinition, ticket: TicketState, planAgent: string | null): Promise<PhaseResult>**

   Dispatches to the appropriate handler based on phase.type:

   **Script phase handler:**
   - Resolves script path: `config.scriptDir + '/' + phase.command`
   - Interpolates args by rendering each arg string through templateRenderer.renderString with the ticket
   - Runs via execCommand with cwd set to the ticket's worktree
   - Success = exitCode 0
   - Runs capture rules after execution

   **Agent phase handler:**
   - Renders the prompt template via templateRenderer.render(phase.promptTemplate, ticket)
   - Resolves the agent via resolveAgent(config, phase.agent, ticket.agent, planAgent)
   - Invokes via invokeAgent with the resolved config
   - Passes logger.agentOutput as the onOutput callback
   - Success = agent exitCode 0
   - Runs capture rules after execution

   **Poll phase handler:**
   - Runs the poll command at intervalSeconds intervals
   - Continues until: command exits 0 (success), or timeoutSeconds exceeded (failure)
   - Between polls, waits using a simple setTimeout/sleep
   - Interpolates args same as script phase
   - Runs capture rules on the final successful execution

   **Terminal phase handler:**
   - Always succeeds
   - If phase.notify is true, returns a flag (the runner will set ticket status to 'needs_attention')
   - nextPhase is always null

   **Capture logic (shared across non-terminal phases):**
   - For each key in phase.capture:
     - If value is 'stdout': capture the phase's stdout output
     - Otherwise: treat value as a shell command template, render it with the ticket context, execute it, capture its stdout
   - Returns all captured values in the result

   **Next phase resolution:**
   - If success: nextPhase = phase.onSuccess ?? null
   - If failure and phase.onFailure === 'retry': nextPhase = phase.id (re-run same phase)
   - If failure and phase.onFailure === 'abort': nextPhase = 'abort' (special value)
   - Otherwise: nextPhase = phase.onFailure ?? null

tasks.name: Runner / State Machine
tasks.description: The main state machine engine. Implements the daemon loop that scans plans, picks up ready tickets, executes phases, and manages transitions.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/core/runner.ts` — the core state machine that drives ticket execution.

Imports from other modules:
- Types: OrchestratorConfig, TicketState, PlanFile from './types.ts'
- Config: loadConfig, resolveAgent from './config.ts'
- State: createStateManager, StateManager from './state.ts'
- Workflow: createWorkflowLoader, WorkflowLoader from './workflow.ts'
- Template: createTemplateRenderer, TemplateRenderer from './template.ts'
- Phases: createPhaseExecutor, PhaseExecutor, PhaseResult from '../phases/executor.ts'
- Logger: createLogger, Logger from '../utils/logger.ts'

Export the following:

1. **createRunner(config: OrchestratorConfig)** — factory that initializes all subsystems and returns a Runner.

2. **Runner** object with methods:

   **tick(): Promise<void>**
   The core single-tick logic:
   a. Resolve dependencies across all active plans (transition queued → ready where blockers complete)
   b. Get count of currently running tickets
   c. Get all ready tickets across active plans
   d. For each ready ticket (up to maxConcurrency - runningCount):
      - Set ticket status to 'running'
      - Start executing the ticket's current phase (don't await — run concurrently)
   e. The tick itself returns after dispatching. Phase execution is fire-and-forget within the tick.

   **executeTicketPhase(ticket: TicketState): Promise<void>**
   Executes the current phase of a ticket:
   a. Load the plan to get planAgent
   b. Load the workflow and get the current phase definition
   c. Log phase start
   d. Check retries: if this phase has exceeded maxRetries, transition to onFailure or 'failed'
   e. Execute the phase via PhaseExecutor
   f. Log phase end (with duration)
   g. Record phase in phaseHistory
   h. Merge captured values into ticket.context
   i. Determine next phase:
      - If result.nextPhase === 'retry': increment retries[phaseId], keep currentPhase the same, set status back to 'ready'
      - If result.nextPhase === 'abort' or null with failure: set status to 'failed'
      - If result.nextPhase is a terminal phase with notify: set status to 'needs_attention'
      - If result.nextPhase is a terminal phase without notify: set status to 'complete'
      - Otherwise: update currentPhase to nextPhase, set status to 'ready' (so next tick picks it up)
   j. Save the updated ticket state
   k. Wrap everything in try/catch — on unexpected error, set ticket to 'failed' with error message

   **runSingleTicket(planId: string, ticketId: string): Promise<void>**
   Runs a single ticket to completion (blocking). Loops: execute phase, check if terminal, repeat. Used by `orchestrator run` command.

   **startDaemon(options?: { signal?: AbortSignal }): Promise<void>**
   Runs tick() in a loop with config.pollInterval seconds between ticks. Respects AbortSignal for graceful shutdown. Logs each tick start.

Key design decisions:
- Phase execution within a tick is concurrent (Promise.all for dispatched tickets)
- But a single ticket's phases are sequential (one phase at a time)
- The runner never makes judgment calls — it follows workflow transitions deterministically
- On any unexpected error, the ticket goes to 'failed' status, never silently drops
- Use a Set<string> to track in-flight ticket IDs to prevent double-dispatch

tasks.name: CLI Entry Point
tasks.description: Commander-based CLI with all orchestrator commands: init, daemon, run, tick, status, pause, resume, skip, retry, and plan-level commands.
tasks.prompt:
Create `src/cli.ts` — the Commander-based CLI entry point for the orchestrator.

Imports:
- commander: Command from 'commander'
- Config: loadConfig from './core/config.ts'
- State: createStateManager from './core/state.ts'
- Runner: createRunner from './core/runner.ts'
- Logger: createLogger from './utils/logger.ts'
- chalk for colored output
- fs/promises and path as needed

Set up the CLI program:
```typescript
const program = new Command()
  .name('orchestrator')
  .description('Agent orchestrator — deterministic state machine for autonomous software development')
  .version('0.1.0');
```

Implement these commands:

**orchestrator init**
- Scaffolds the directory structure: state/, logs/, workflows/, prompts/, scripts/, skills/
- Copies default orchestrator.yaml if it doesn't exist
- Prints a summary of created directories

**orchestrator daemon [--concurrency <n>] [--agent <name>]**
- Loads config, optionally overrides maxConcurrency and defaultAgent from CLI flags
- Creates runner and calls startDaemon()
- Sets up SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers for graceful shutdown via AbortController
- Prints 'Daemon started, polling every Ns' message

**orchestrator run <planId> <ticketId>**
- Loads config, creates runner, calls runSingleTicket(planId, ticketId)
- Blocks until the ticket reaches a terminal state
- Prints final status

**orchestrator tick**
- Loads config, creates runner, calls tick() once, then exits

**orchestrator status [--plan <planId>] [--json]**
- Loads all plans and their tickets from state manager
- Without --json: prints a formatted table showing plan name, ticket ID, status, current phase
- With --json: outputs raw JSON
- With --plan: filters to just that plan
- Use chalk for colored status indicators (green=complete, yellow=running, red=failed, gray=queued)

**orchestrator pause <planId> <ticketId>**
- Sets ticket status to 'paused' via state manager

**orchestrator resume <planId> <ticketId>**
- Sets ticket status to 'ready' (so next tick picks it up)

**orchestrator skip <planId> <ticketId> <phase>**
- Sets ticket's currentPhase to the specified phase, status to 'ready'

**orchestrator retry <planId> <ticketId>**
- Resets the retry counter for the current phase, sets status to 'ready'

**orchestrator pause-plan <planId>**
- Sets plan status to 'paused'

**orchestrator resume-plan <planId>**
- Sets plan status to 'active'

End with `program.parse()`. Add `#!/usr/bin/env node` at the top for the bin entry point.

Keep each command handler concise — load config, create the relevant manager, do the operation, print result.

tasks.name: Standard Workflow YAML
tasks.description: The default 'standard' workflow YAML defining the full pipeline: setup → implement → self_review → simplify → verify → create_pr → review cycle → merge → cleanup → complete.
tasks.prompt:
Create `workflows/standard.yaml` — the default feature implementation workflow.

This YAML defines a WorkflowDefinition with the following phase graph:

```
setup → implement → self_review → simplify → verify → create_pr
  → await_review ←→ handle_review → verify_post_review → push_fixes
  → await_merge → cleanup → complete
```

Define each phase:

```yaml
name: standard
description: "Full implementation workflow: setup → implement → self-review → simplify → verify → create PR → review cycle → merge → cleanup."
phases:
```

1. **setup** (script): Creates git worktree
   - command: setup-worktree.sh
   - args: ["{{repo}}", "{{branch}}", "{{worktree}}"]
   - onSuccess: implement
   - onFailure: abort

2. **implement** (agent): Implement the feature
   - promptTemplate: implement.md
   - allowedTools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash", "Grep", "Glob"]
   - maxTurns: 50
   - maxRetries: 2
   - capture: { git_diff_stat: "git -C {{worktree}} diff main --stat" }
   - onSuccess: self_review
   - onFailure: retry

3. **self_review** (agent): Review own diff (read-only)
   - promptTemplate: self-review.md
   - allowedTools: ["Read", "Bash", "Grep", "Glob"]
   - maxTurns: 10
   - capture: { review_verdict: "stdout" }
   - onSuccess: simplify
   - onFailure: implement  (sends back to implement on FAIL verdict)

4. **simplify** (agent): Clean up implementation
   - promptTemplate: simplify.md
   - allowedTools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash", "Grep", "Glob"]
   - maxTurns: 20
   - onSuccess: verify
   - onFailure: verify  (proceed to verify even if simplify has issues)

5. **verify** (agent): Run tests and linting
   - promptTemplate: verify.md
   - allowedTools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash", "Grep", "Glob"]
   - maxTurns: 30
   - maxRetries: 2
   - capture: { test_output: "stdout" }
   - onSuccess: create_pr
   - onFailure: retry

6. **create_pr** (agent): Push and open PR
   - promptTemplate: create-pr.md
   - allowedTools: ["Bash"]
   - maxTurns: 10
   - capture:
     pr_url: "gh -C {{worktree}} pr view --json url -q .url"
     pr_number: "gh -C {{worktree}} pr view --json number -q .number"
   - onSuccess: await_review
   - onFailure: escalate

7. **await_review** (poll): Wait for PR review
   - command: check-pr-review.sh
   - args: ["{{repo}}", "{{pr_number}}"]
   - intervalSeconds: 120
   - timeoutSeconds: 86400
   - onSuccess: handle_review
   - onFailure: escalate

8. **handle_review** (agent): Address review comments
   - promptTemplate: handle-review.md
   - allowedTools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash", "Grep", "Glob"]
   - maxTurns: 30
   - onSuccess: verify_post_review
   - onFailure: escalate

9. **verify_post_review** (agent): Re-run verification after fixes
   - promptTemplate: verify.md
   - allowedTools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash", "Grep", "Glob"]
   - maxTurns: 30
   - maxRetries: 1
   - onSuccess: push_fixes
   - onFailure: retry

10. **push_fixes** (agent): Push fix commits, back to review
    - promptTemplate: push-fixes.md
    - allowedTools: ["Bash"]
    - maxTurns: 5
    - onSuccess: await_review  (creates the review cycle loop)
    - onFailure: escalate

11. **await_merge** (poll): Wait for PR merge
    - command: check-pr-merged.sh
    - args: ["{{repo}}", "{{pr_number}}"]
    - intervalSeconds: 120
    - timeoutSeconds: 86400
    - onSuccess: cleanup
    - onFailure: escalate

12. **cleanup** (script): Remove worktree
    - command: cleanup-worktree.sh
    - args: ["{{worktree}}"]
    - onSuccess: complete
    - onFailure: complete  (cleanup failure shouldn't block completion)

13. **complete** (terminal): Done
    - notify: false

14. **escalate** (terminal): Needs human attention
    - notify: true

15. **abort** (terminal): Setup failed
    - notify: true

Note on the review cycle: await_review succeeds when there are review comments to address (→ handle_review). When the PR is approved with no unresolved threads, check-pr-review.sh should exit with code 2 or similar to indicate 'approved' — the workflow needs to handle this by routing approved PRs to await_merge. One approach: have check-pr-review.sh exit 0 for 'has comments', exit 1 for 'timeout/error', and capture a 'review_status' value. The runner can check this. Alternatively, simplify: when check-pr-review.sh detects approval, have it exit 0 and set a capture value, then handle_review checks if there are actually comments to address. Keep it pragmatic.

tasks.name: Bugfix Workflow YAML
tasks.description: Abbreviated workflow for bug fixes that skips self-review and simplify phases.
tasks.prompt:
Create `workflows/bugfix.yaml` — an abbreviated workflow for bug fixes.

This skips self_review and simplify since bug fixes should be minimal and targeted.

Phase graph:
```
setup → implement → verify → create_pr → await_review ←→ handle_review → verify_post_review → push_fixes → await_merge → cleanup → complete
```

```yaml
name: bugfix
description: "Abbreviated workflow for bug fixes. Skips self-review and simplify for minimal, targeted changes."
phases:
```

1. **setup** (script): Same as standard
   - command: setup-worktree.sh
   - args: ["{{repo}}", "{{branch}}", "{{worktree}}"]
   - onSuccess: implement
   - onFailure: abort

2. **implement** (agent): Bug fix implementation
   - promptTemplate: implement-bugfix.md  (uses bugfix-specific prompt)
   - allowedTools: ["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash", "Grep", "Glob"]
   - maxTurns: 30  (lower than standard — bug fixes should be smaller)
   - maxRetries: 2
   - capture: { git_diff_stat: "git -C {{worktree}} diff main --stat" }
   - onSuccess: verify  (skips directly to verify)
   - onFailure: retry

3. **verify** through **complete/escalate/abort**: Same as standard workflow

Include the same terminal phases: complete, escalate, abort.

tasks.name: Workflow Registry & Docs
tasks.description: The workflow registry YAML that lists available workflows, plus a README documenting the phase type reference for workflow authors.
tasks.prompt:
Create two files:

**1. workflows/registry.yaml**

A YAML array listing all available workflows:

```yaml
- name: standard
  file: standard.yaml
  description: >
    Full implementation workflow: setup → implement → self-review → simplify →
    verify → create PR → review cycle → merge → cleanup.
  tags: [feature, default]

- name: bugfix
  file: bugfix.yaml
  description: >
    Abbreviated workflow for bug fixes. Skips self-review and simplify.
    Uses focused prompts emphasizing minimal changes and root cause identification.
  tags: [bug, hotfix]
```

**2. workflows/README.md**

A reference document for workflow authors. Cover:

- **Overview**: What workflows are, how the state machine uses them
- **Phase types reference**: For each type (script, agent, poll, terminal), document all fields, required vs optional, and give an example
- **Template variables**: List all available {{variables}} from ticket state and context, explain how captures add new variables
- **Capture rules**: How to define captures, the special 'stdout' value, how captured values flow to later phases
- **Transitions**: How onSuccess/onFailure work, the special 'retry' and 'abort' values
- **Registry**: How to add a new workflow (create YAML + add registry entry)
- **Tips**: Common patterns like review cycles (back-edges in the graph), read-only agent phases, retry limits

Keep it practical and example-heavy. This is what the LLM planner reads to understand how to create custom workflows.

tasks.name: Prompt Templates
tasks.description: All Nunjucks markdown prompt templates used by agent phases. These are the prompts sent to headless coding agents.
tasks.prompt:
Create all prompt template files in the `prompts/` directory. These are Nunjucks markdown templates that get rendered with ticket variables and sent to headless coding agents.

Available template variables (from ticket state):
- {{ticket_id}}, {{title}}, {{description}}, {{acceptance_criteria_list}}
- {{repo}}, {{branch}}, {{worktree}}, {{linear_url}}, {{plan_id}}
- Any captured context values from previous phases: {{git_diff_stat}}, {{pr_url}}, {{pr_number}}, {{test_output}}, {{review_verdict}}

IMPORTANT: These prompts are sent to headless coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) that have access to the TARGET PROJECT's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. The prompts should NOT include project-specific build/test commands — the agent discovers those from the project's own docs.

---

**prompts/implement.md**
You are implementing a feature for ticket {{ticket_id}}.

Title: {{title}}

Description:
{{description}}

Acceptance Criteria:
{{acceptance_criteria_list}}

Instructions:
1. Read the project's CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md to understand conventions, build commands, and test commands
2. Explore the codebase to understand the relevant architecture
3. Implement the feature according to the description and acceptance criteria
4. Write tests that cover the acceptance criteria
5. Run the project's test suite and fix any failures
6. Run the project's linter and fix any issues
7. Commit your changes with a clear commit message referencing {{ticket_id}}

Do not create unnecessary abstractions. Keep changes focused and minimal.

---

**prompts/implement-bugfix.md**
You are fixing a bug for ticket {{ticket_id}}.

Title: {{title}}

Description:
{{description}}

Instructions:
1. Read the project's CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md to understand conventions
2. Identify the root cause of the bug — do not just treat symptoms
3. Make the minimal change needed to fix the bug
4. Write a test that reproduces the bug and verifies the fix
5. Run the project's test suite and fix any failures
6. Run the project's linter
7. Commit with a clear message referencing {{ticket_id}}

Keep changes minimal and targeted. Do not refactor surrounding code.

---

**prompts/self-review.md**
You are reviewing the diff for ticket {{ticket_id}}: {{title}}

Run `git diff main` to see all changes.

Review the diff against this checklist:
- [ ] Correctness: Does the code do what the ticket asks?
- [ ] Error handling: Are edge cases and errors handled?
- [ ] Security: Any injection, auth, or data exposure issues?
- [ ] Test coverage: Are the acceptance criteria tested?
- [ ] Naming: Are names clear and consistent with the codebase?
- [ ] Complexity: Is there unnecessary complexity that could be simplified?
- [ ] Dead code: Is there commented-out code or unused imports?

If ALL checks pass, output exactly: VERDICT: PASS
If ANY check fails, output: VERDICT: FAIL followed by a brief explanation of what needs fixing.

Do NOT modify any files. This is a read-only review.

---

**prompts/simplify.md**
You are simplifying the implementation for ticket {{ticket_id}}: {{title}}

Review the diff with `git diff main` and clean up:
- Remove dead code, commented-out code, unused imports
- Collapse unnecessary abstractions (inline single-use helpers)
- Simplify conditionals and control flow
- Remove over-engineering (feature flags, unnecessary configurability)

Do NOT change functionality. Do NOT add features. Just clean up.
Run tests after simplifying to ensure nothing broke.
Commit any changes.

---

**prompts/verify.md**
You are verifying the implementation for ticket {{ticket_id}}: {{title}}

1. Read the project's CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md to find test and lint commands
2. Run the full test suite
3. Run the linter
4. Run any other verification steps mentioned in the project docs
5. If anything fails, fix it and re-run
6. Commit any fixes

All tests and checks must pass before this phase can succeed.

---

**prompts/create-pr.md**
Create a pull request for ticket {{ticket_id}}: {{title}}

1. Push the branch: `git push -u origin {{branch}}`
2. Create a PR using `gh pr create` with:
   - Title: "{{ticket_id}}: {{title}}"
   - Body that includes:
     - Summary of changes (from the diff)
     - Acceptance criteria from the ticket
     - Test coverage summary
     {% if linear_url %}- Link to ticket: {{linear_url}}{% endif %}

Do not merge the PR. Just create it.

---

**prompts/handle-review.md**
Address PR review comments for ticket {{ticket_id}}: {{title}}

PR: {{pr_url}}

1. Read all pending review comments: `gh pr view {{pr_number}} --comments`
2. Also check inline review threads: `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{{pr_number}}/comments`
3. For each unresolved comment:
   - Understand what the reviewer is asking
   - Make the requested change (or explain why not in a reply)
   - Resolve the thread if the fix is straightforward
4. Commit all changes with a message like "Address review feedback for {{ticket_id}}"

---

**prompts/push-fixes.md**
Push review fix commits for ticket {{ticket_id}}.

1. Run `git status` to verify there are committed changes to push
2. Push: `git push origin {{branch}}`
3. Verify the push succeeded

Do not force push. If push fails, investigate and fix.

---

For all templates: use clean markdown formatting, keep instructions numbered and actionable, and reference {{variables}} where appropriate.

tasks.name: Infrastructure Bash Scripts
tasks.description: Bash scripts for deterministic infrastructure operations: worktree setup/cleanup, PR review checking, PR merge checking.
tasks.prompt:
Create four bash scripts in the `scripts/` directory. All scripts should be executable (chmod +x), use `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, include `set -euo pipefail`, and output clear status messages.

---

**scripts/setup-worktree.sh**
Args: $1=repo_path, $2=branch_name, $3=worktree_path

Steps:
1. Validate all three args are provided
2. cd into the repo path
3. Fetch latest from origin: `git fetch origin`
4. Create a new worktree: `git worktree add "$3" -b "$2" origin/main`
   - If branch already exists, try `git worktree add "$3" "$2"` instead
5. Verify the worktree was created
6. Print success message with worktree path

Exit 0 on success, exit 1 on any failure.

---

**scripts/cleanup-worktree.sh**
Args: $1=worktree_path

Steps:
1. Validate arg
2. Remove the worktree: `git worktree remove "$1" --force`
3. Prune: `git worktree prune`
4. Print success message

Exit 0 on success, exit 1 on failure. Don't fail if worktree doesn't exist (idempotent).

---

**scripts/check-pr-review.sh**
Args: $1=repo_path, $2=pr_number

This script checks the state of PR reviews. It needs to distinguish three states:
- **Has unresolved review comments** → exit 0 (success — there's work to do)
- **Approved with no unresolved threads** → exit 0 but write 'approved' to stdout
- **Still waiting (no reviews yet)** → exit 1 (keep polling)
- **Error** → exit 2

Use `gh` CLI:
```bash
# Get review decision
review_state=$(gh pr view "$2" --repo "$1" --json reviewDecision -q .reviewDecision)

# Get pending review threads
comments=$(gh pr view "$2" --repo "$1" --json comments -q '.comments | length')
```

Logic:
- If reviewDecision is 'APPROVED': echo 'approved' and exit 0
- If reviewDecision is 'CHANGES_REQUESTED' or there are unresolved comments: echo 'changes_requested' and exit 0
- Otherwise (REVIEW_REQUIRED, empty, etc.): exit 1 (keep polling)

---

**scripts/check-pr-merged.sh**
Args: $1=repo_path, $2=pr_number

Check if a PR has been merged.

Use `gh`:
```bash
state=$(gh pr view "$2" --repo "$1" --json state -q .state)
```

- If state is 'MERGED': echo 'merged' and exit 0
- If state is 'CLOSED' (but not merged): echo 'closed' and exit 2 (error — PR was closed without merge)
- Otherwise: exit 1 (keep polling)

---

All scripts should include helpful error messages on invalid args. Use `echo` for status output (stdout is captured by the orchestrator).

tasks.name: CLAUDE.md & Project Documentation
tasks.description: The orchestrator repo's own CLAUDE.md with progressive disclosure, orienting both coding agents and the LLM planner.
tasks.prompt:
Create `CLAUDE.md` for the agent-orchestrator repo. This serves two audiences:
1. Coding agents working ON the orchestrator (adding features, fixing bugs)
2. The LLM planner running INSIDE the orchestrator repo

Structure with progressive disclosure:

## Quick Reference (top-level, always visible)
- What this project is (1 sentence)
- Language: TypeScript, built with commander/zod/nunjucks/yaml
- Build: `npm run build`, Dev: `npm run dev -- <command>`
- Test: (no test framework yet — manual testing with state files)
- Key commands: `orchestrator daemon`, `orchestrator run <plan> <ticket>`, `orchestrator status`

## Architecture
- Two-process design: LLM planner writes state files, state machine reads and executes
- State files in `state/` are the only contract between planner and runner
- Workflows are YAML-defined directed graphs of phases
- Four phase types: script, agent, poll, terminal
- Agent-agnostic: Claude Code or Codex, configurable at 4 priority levels

## Project Structure
- src/core/ — Config, state, workflow, template, runner, types
- src/agents/ — Agent invocation abstraction
- src/phases/ — Phase type executors
- src/utils/ — Shell execution, logging
- workflows/ — YAML workflow definitions
- prompts/ — Nunjucks markdown templates for agent phases
- scripts/ — Bash scripts for infrastructure phases
- state/ — Runtime state files (gitignored)
- logs/ — Per-ticket logs (gitignored)

## Conventions
- Zod schemas in types.ts are the single source of truth for all data shapes
- Named exports everywhere, no default exports
- async/await, fs/promises, no callbacks
- Runner is deterministic — NEVER makes judgment calls, follows YAML transitions
- Template variables use {{variable}} syntax
- State files are pretty-printed JSON with 2-space indent

## For the LLM Planner
- See skills/planner/SKILL.md for how to create plans and ticket state files
- See workflows/README.md for the workflow authoring reference
- See skills/providers/ for tool-specific ticket fetching (Linear, GitHub Issues)

## Adding a New Workflow
1. Create a YAML file in workflows/
2. Add an entry to workflows/registry.yaml
3. See workflows/README.md for phase type reference

## Adding a New Agent Provider
1. Add the agent config to orchestrator.yaml under `agents:`
2. Add CLI translation logic in src/agents/invoke.ts

Keep it concise but complete. The CLAUDE.md should make it possible for a fresh agent to understand the project and start contributing immediately.

tasks.name: Agent Skills Documentation
tasks.description: SKILL.md files for the planner, workflow authoring, and project management tool providers.
tasks.prompt:
Create all SKILL.md files in the skills/ directory. These are progressive-disclosure documents that teach the LLM planner how to use the orchestrator.

---

**skills/planner/SKILL.md**

This is the core skill. Teach the LLM planner how to:

1. **Create a plan directory**: `state/{plan-id}/` with a `_plan.json` file
2. **Write _plan.json**: Full schema with all fields explained, examples
   - id: slug matching directory name
   - name: human-readable
   - repo: absolute path to target project
   - workflow: name from registry (default: 'standard')
   - agent: optional override (default: null, uses global)
   - worktreeRoot: where worktrees will be created
   - status: start as 'active'
   - tickets: array with ticketId, order, blockedBy
3. **Write ticket state files**: Full schema with all fields explained
   - Everything above 'status' is written by planner
   - Everything from 'status' down is managed by runner
   - Initial status should be 'queued' (or 'ready' if no blockers)
   - currentPhase should be the first phase of the workflow (e.g., 'setup')
4. **Dependency management**: How blockedBy works, scoping within plans
5. **Workflow selection**: How to read the registry and choose the right workflow
6. **Agent selection**: When to override the agent at plan or ticket level
7. **CLI commands**: How the human uses orchestrator CLI to manage execution

Include 2-3 complete examples: creating a plan from scratch, creating tickets with dependencies.

---

**skills/workflows/SKILL.md**

Teach the LLM planner and developers how to create custom workflows:

1. Phase type reference (script, agent, poll, terminal)
2. All fields for each phase type
3. Template variable system
4. Capture rules
5. Transition logic (onSuccess, onFailure, retry, abort)
6. How to add to the registry
7. Example: creating a 'review-only' workflow that just creates a PR for existing changes

---

**skills/providers/linear/SKILL.md**

Teach the planner how to work with Linear:

1. Fetching tickets via Linear MCP or API
2. Mapping Linear fields to ticket state fields:
   - Issue title → title
   - Issue description → description
   - Labels/acceptance criteria → acceptanceCriteria
   - Issue URL → linearUrl
   - Issue relations → blockedBy dependencies
3. Naming conventions for branches: `{username}/{ticket-id}-{slug}`
4. Example: fetching a Linear sprint and generating plan + ticket files

---

**skills/providers/github-issues/SKILL.md**

Teach the planner how to work with GitHub Issues:

1. Fetching issues via `gh issue list` and `gh issue view`
2. Mapping GitHub fields to ticket state fields:
   - Issue title → title
   - Issue body → description (parse acceptance criteria from body template)
   - Issue URL → linearUrl (reuse the field)
   - Issue references (#123 mentions) → blockedBy dependencies
3. Naming conventions
4. Example: fetching issues by milestone and generating plan + ticket files

---

All skills should use clear headers, include JSON/YAML examples, and be written for an LLM reader (clear, unambiguous, no assumed context).

dependencyGraph.description: Visual overview of task dependencies. Tasks at the same tier can run in parallel.

tier0_foundation.note: Must complete first — creates package.json and installs deps

tier1_types_and_content.note: Types depend on scaffolding. All content tasks (13-19) have zero code deps and can start immediately.

tier2_utilities_and_core.note: All depend on types. Shell and logger also depend on scaffolding. Can all run in parallel.

tier4_execution.note: Depends on types, shell, config, template, and agent invoker

tier5_orchestration.note: Depends on all core modules and phase executor

tier6_cli.note: Depends on runner, state, and config

parallelizationStrategy.maxParallelAgents: Up to 12 tasks can run in the first two tiers alone
parallelizationStrategy.quickWins: Tasks 13-19 (content) can start immediately and have no code dependencies on each other
parallelizationStrategy.integrationNote: After all tasks complete, a final integration pass should: 1) run npm run build to verify TypeScript compilation, 2) create a sample state/ plan and run `orchestrator tick` to smoke test, 3) fix any import/interface mismatches between modules
